methodology.sourcePolicy: Applied docs/10 source hierarchy: project baselines and metadata-only academic entries can route review, but cannot alone convert claims into accepted production facts.
biasChecks: unsupported certainty in dates, names, titles, clothing, symbols, and institutions
methodology.sourceStatementBoundary: This review distinguishes what the project baseline/source records state, what scholarly interpretation still requires page verification, and what this reviewer infers for game abstraction.
methodology.fullTextLimit: Academic candidate sources in the reviewed content remain BIBLIOGRAPHIC_METADATA_ONLY or SOURCE_DISCOVERY_ONLY where pageOrSection is RESEARCH REQUIRED; this review does not claim full-text verification for those sources.

inputsReviewed.use: source, claim-record, uncertainty, bias, and violence-cost policy
source discovery and candidate bibliography limits
stable IDs, source fields, localization, event-data, and validation expectations
M7 Beta scenario, person, title, event, localization, source, and claim records
M7 Beta audio, art, localization, viewport, static-resource, and unresolved-risk coverage
closed tutorial/hint/encyclopedia evidence tied to accepted content-source records
independent QA evidence that guidance fixtures match the accepted content-source set

inputsReviewed.locator: Prompt A and Prompt B sections on UI/localization/visual direction
inputsReviewed.use: product-owner directive for future UI/localization/visual quality-track constraints
related art/UX handoff dependency and boundary record
returned art/visual thread handoff envelope; schema-valid but still pending systems/QA review
structured visual/UX/localization guidance, including debug/player separation, asset slots, and cultural-symbol risks

reviewClaimRecords.claim: The reviewed M7 Beta content set is suitable as culture-review evidence, but it is not formal content lock acceptance and does not accept page-unverified real-world facts.

source_passages.summary: Formal claims need claim records and AI research must preserve uncertainty rather than invent evidence.
The baseline has zero accepted Beta real-world claims and marks itself as not content lock acceptance.
The scenario fill set marks formal content lock as a future Human Gate and keeps names, titles, chronology, and participants research-required.

competing_interpretations: A future content-lock task may accept a subset after page-level source review and Human Gate approval.
This review only accepts the evidence chain and risk routing.
reviewClaimRecords.game_abstraction: Permit use as Beta validation and review evidence; do not present it as final historical truth.

reviewClaimRecords.claim: The M7 art/UX handoff is a dependency for later UI beautification tasks, not a substitute for culture review or historical source review.

source_passages.locator: Prompt A goals and visual/localization sections
source_passages.summary: The directive adds a UI/localization/visual quality track and requests structured handoff before UI beautification implementation.
The related task requires concept-art mapping, 1.0 versus post-1.0 decisions, reviewer acceptance, and no implementation work.
The returned handoff separates current mappings from target expression, flags cultural-symbol risk, requires i18n, hides debug labels from player mode, and keeps manual node battle out of 1.0.

competing_interpretations: Visual direction can guide implementation after review.
It cannot authorize unreviewed names, symbols, sacred imagery, cultural costumes, UI metaphors, or new systems.
reviewClaimRecords.game_abstraction: Future UI tasks must consume reviewed read models, stable localization keys, neutral placeholders, and explicit asset slots.

acceptedFindings.reviewerRationale: The baseline correctly creates gates and uncertainty records, records zero accepted Beta real-world claims, and blocks metadata-only sources from becoming production facts.
The scenario/person/event set keeps scenario anchors, persons, and titles as composite or research-required records and does not assert page-verified chronology, biography, or identity mechanics.

The coercion/population-loss hook records victim groups and source regions as RESEARCH REQUIRED, lists immediate and long-term consequences, and blocks reward framing behind Human Gate rather than minimizing harm.

Tutorial, hint, and encyclopedia surfaces are accepted only as generated evidence tied to the accepted content-source graph and QA drift tests; they must continue exposing review labels instead of flattening uncertainty.

Neutral static audio/art slots are acceptable as coverage evidence because their notes disclaim period instruments, melodies, sacred sound, flags, scripts, temple/manuscript/court-symbol claims, and culture-specific production assertions.

The directive and returned art/UX handoff are accepted as future M7 UI/localization/visual quality-track constraints. They do not replace this culture review and do not authorize UI implementation, new systems, or unreviewed cultural assets before systems/QA review.

requestChangeBeforeLockFindings.requiredChange: Before formal content lock, page- or section-verified source records must be added for exact scenario chronology, participants, monsoon campaign claims, and scenario display hooks, or those records must remain explicitly RESEARCH REQUIRED.
requestChangeBeforeLockFindings.reviewerRationale: The current file handles this correctly for Beta evidence, but formal lock cannot accept these anchors as historical facts from project bibliography metadata alone.

requestChangeBeforeLockFindings.requiredChange: Before public-facing lock, names, titles, aliases, transliterations, and title glosses need language/style-guide records with source forms or must stay generic and visibly review-blocked.
requestChangeBeforeLockFindings.reviewerRationale: The current generic strings avoid fabricating local titles, but they are not a final language solution.

requestChangeBeforeLockFindings.requiredChange: Run final zh-Hans language-quality review for player-facing wording, names, titles, tooltips, and long English/Chinese layout effects before content lock.
requestChangeBeforeLockFindings.reviewerRationale: Coverage exists, but quality review and sensitive terminology remain explicitly pending.

requestChangeBeforeLockFindings.requiredChange: Future UI beautification must treat the returned structured art/UX handoff as pending systems/QA review, and must map concept art to current systems without adding unimplemented 1.0 systems.
requestChangeBeforeLockFindings.reviewerRationale: Concept-art mapping is necessary for visual quality, but even the returned handoff cannot bypass historical/cultural review or turn target expression into product fact.

requestChangeBeforeLockFindings.requiredChange: Future player-mode UI must hide debug/prototype labels, raw IDs, raw reason codes, state hashes, M2/M3 internal labels, and test-only names; player-facing strings must route through i18n and reviewed terminology.
requestChangeBeforeLockFindings.reviewerRationale: Debug vocabulary can leak unsupported modern/technical framing and undermine cultural review if exposed as player-facing language.

blockedForProductionFindings.blockedUse: Formal acceptance of scenario years, chronology, participants, or named historical persons as verified facts.
blockedForProductionFindings.reviewerRationale: The current records are intentionally composite, low-confidence, and review-blocked; production factual acceptance would need new claim records with page/section evidence.

blockedForProductionFindings.blockedUse: Culture-specific clothing, architecture, weapons, flags, scripts, religious imagery, music, instruments, court ritual sound, or sacred UI symbols.
blockedForProductionFindings.reviewerRationale: Neutral placeholders are allowed; culture-specific production assets require period, region, and usage evidence plus cultural review.

blockedForProductionFindings.blockedUse: Any mechanic, UI copy, or event option that frames coercive population movement, slavery, famine, massacre, religious conflict, or wartime loss as cost-free reward.
blockedForProductionFindings.reviewerRationale: The record correctly lists victim/source-region research requirements and long-term consequences; production reward framing is blocked unless those costs remain explicit and reviewed.

blockedForProductionFindings.blockedUse: Manual node battle UI, battle implementation, or 1.0 scope reversal through visual design or concept-art interpretation.
blockedForProductionFindings.reviewerRationale: Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE and outside 1.0 mainline; visual handoff cannot reverse a product-owner Human Gate decision.

humanGateRequiredFindings.reviewerRationale: This writer review supplies evidence for QA and later lock review only; it cannot mark ACCEPTED/CLOSED or approve formal content lock.

humanGateRequiredFindings.gate: major cultural-risk decision for coercion, forced movement, slavery, famine, massacre, religious conflict, or wartime population loss
humanGateRequiredFindings.reviewerRationale: High-risk violence-cost content is present as a blocking review hook, not an accepted gameplay reward.

humanGateRequiredFindings.gate: culture-specific art, music, symbol, sacred image, ritual, clothing, architecture, weapon, flag, script, name, or title production choice
humanGateRequiredFindings.reviewerRationale: Neutral placeholders do not license production cultural assets. Major unresolved visual/language/symbol choices need Human Gate after source and culture review.

humanGateRequiredFindings.gate: identity shortcut or fixed culture/religion/polity buff exception
humanGateRequiredFindings.reviewerRationale: The current content avoids identity mechanics; any exception would be a major cultural-risk decision.

humanGateRequiredFindings.gate: manual node battle reversal or formal 1.0 scope reversal
humanGateRequiredFindings.reviewerRationale: Visual/UX mapping must explicitly label manual node battle UI as not in the 1.0 mainline unless a later Human Gate changes the decision.

uiLocalizationVisualConstraintsForFuture.constraint: Map every concept-art element to implemented capability, prototype UI, designed-but-unsurfaced capability, target expression only, post-1.0, or rejected-for-1.0; do not infer new systems.
Keep neutral placeholder symbols until source-specific cultural review approves any culture-specific script, sacred image, flag, icon, clothing, architecture, weapon, or ritual motif.
All player-facing text must route through stable localization keys with source notes, context, character limits, and reviewed terminology; no hard-coded historical names or titles.
Hide state hashes, internal IDs, raw reason codes, milestone labels, prototype labels, test names, and debug timing from player mode; debug overlay may remain developer-only.
Do not create manual node battle UI or imply 1.0 inclusion through art/UX; label it not in 1.0 mainline unless later Human Gate reverses DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE.
Player-facing UI may present reviewed read-model data and localized reason text; it must not treat Pixi, React, concept art, or asset slots as authoritative historical or simulation state.

overallVerdict.rationale: The reviewed set is coherent Beta culture/history/language evidence with explicit uncertainty, source limits, violence-cost handling, and Human Gate routing. It should proceed to QA review, while formal content lock and major cultural-risk decisions remain outside writer authority.
